title: Space Tech Briefing — May 22, 2026
summary:
Good morning, space enthusiasts! It's Friday, May 22nd, 2026, and you're tuned into the Space Technology Morning Briefing, your daily dose of cosmic innovation and exploration! I'm your host, ready to launch you into the latest headlines from across the galaxy and right here on Earth.

First up, a fantastic initiative taking flight today! Right here on Earth, an incredible 260,000 students are embarking on the ChlorISS experiment. This massive educational project, led by the French space agency CNES and involving astronaut Sophie Adenot, kicks off on May 22nd. Students will dive deep into understanding how gravity and light influence plant growth. What an exciting way to inspire the next generation of space scientists and botanists! A truly stellar start for 4,500 classes across the nation.

And speaking of stellar journeys, as we wait for today's new launches, let's look back at a major recent development in deep space exploration. NASA's Psyche probe, launched way back in October 2023, just made a crucial maneuver! On May 15th, it successfully performed a gravity assist around Mars. This slingshot maneuver was absolutely vital, boosting its speed and correcting its trajectory. Psyche is now accelerating on its incredible 2.4-billion-kilometer journey towards its target: the metallic asteroid 16-Psyche. It's set to achieve orbit around that unique celestial body in July 2029. Talk about long-haul travel!

And that's your Space Technology Morning Briefing for May 22nd, 2026. Keep looking up, keep exploring, and we'll be back tomorrow with more out-of-this-world news. Have a fantastic day!
